mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated unemployment forces central banks to deploy unconventional easing measures, compressing risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns with a win rate above 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly measures aggregate economic output expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth readings precede negative 20-day market returns in >60% of quarterly observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor market improvement signal produces negative 60-day returns in >58% of triggered months during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation alongside expanding industrial production triggers expectations of monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY and INDPRO growth > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: The inflation-supply combination fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.
